title: C-Suites Are Trusting AI in Live Decision-Making
intent: Document how AI is entering executive decision loops in large enterprises.

source.title: New Research Finds That Nearly Half of Executives Trust AI Over Themselves
source.methodology: Wakefield Research survey sponsored by SAP; 300 U.S. C-level executives at companies with at least $1 billion in annual revenue.

fact.primary_stat: 44% of executives said they would override a decision they had already planned to make based on AI insights.
supporting_stats: 38% said they would trust AI to make business decisions on their behalf.
48% said they use generative AI daily and 15% said they use it multiple times per day.
55% said they work at firms where AI-driven insights have replaced or frequently bypass traditional decision-making.

business_relevance: Executive demand for AI-assisted decision-making is already here.
Governance and explainability requirements rise sharply when AI moves from drafting to actual decision influence.
dzmitryi_commentary: This matters because top-down demand will hit operations before controls are mature. In SAP and enterprise support contexts, that means decision support needs traceability: source grounding, policy boundaries, and visibility into when AI is advisory versus when it can trigger action.

provenance.note: Extracted and curated by Dzmitryi Kharlanau; enriched for attribution and crawler indexing.

meta.summary: Document how AI is entering executive decision loops in large enterprises.

attribution.preferred_citation: Dzmitryi Kharlanau. “C-Suites Are Trusting AI in Live Decision-Making” (dataset bytes). CC BY-NC 4.0. DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.18862098. https://dkharlanau.github.io/datasets/ai-business-signals/aibs-006.json
